flags.concern: Step 7 does not actually establish a single owner for clip movement. Removing `onActionMove*` while keeping the library draggable leaves `@xzdarcy/react-timeline-editor` free to mutate action positions and drive `onChange`, so the library and `useClipDrag` would still compete.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx` still renders `<Timeline>` in draggable mode and the plan does not disable library dragging. In `node_modules/@xzdarcy/react-timeline-editor/src/components/edit_area/edit_action.tsx`, `handleDragEnd` updates `action.start/end` and calls `setEditorData(editorData)` even if no `onActionMoveStart/onActionMoving/onActionMoveEnd` callbacks are supplied; those callbacks are optional glue, not the drag switch. The current custom path also captures on `pointerdown`/`setPointerCapture` in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts`, but the library still uses `onMouseDown` on its drag surface (`edit_action.tsx`), so Step 6/7 needs an explicit ownership mechanism, not just callback removal.

flags.concern: The new indicator state is placed too high in the tree. `useDragIndicatorState()` in `TimelineEditor.tsx` would rerender the full timeline editor on every drag update, which is a likely performance regression from the current imperative DOM path.
flags.evidence: Step 3 mounts indicator state in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx`, the same component that renders `<Timeline>`, track labels, `getActionRender`, and thumbnail maps. Step 4 then drives updates from `useClipDrag` on every `pointermove`; that hook currently has no RAF throttling for internal drags (`src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts`). Today both indicator systems avoid React rerenders by mutating DOM directly. Moving that hot-path state into the parent timeline component is likely to churn the whole editor during drag.

flags.concern: Over-engineering: Step 14 expands `getEffectivePaneHeights` even though the shared allocator already accounts for both top/bottom panes. The repository evidence points to the local subtraction in `EditorPaneTab` as the bug, so changing the allocator API adds unnecessary policy surface.
flags.evidence: `src/shared/contexts/PanesContext.tsx` already computes `effectiveEditorPaneHeight` and `effectiveGenerationsPaneHeight` together when both panes are visible, and `src/shared/contexts/__tests__/PanesContext.test.tsx` asserts that their totals match the viewport across ideal-fit, shrink-to-fit, and degraded tiers. `src/features/editor/components/EditorPaneTab.tsx` then subtracts `effectiveGenerationsPaneHeight` again (`generationsOffset` / `adjustedEditorPaneHeight`). Based on the current repository, removing that local adjustment is the obvious fix; adding `editorPaneOverlapsGenerations` to `getEffectivePaneHeights` looks broader than necessary.
